title: lonicera villosa var. tonsa
intro: Lonicera villosa var. tonsa, also known as the hairy or dwarf Honeysuckle, is a plant species belonging to the Lonicera genus and is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lonicera villosa var. tonsa include its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations of Lonicera villosa var. tonsa typically involve drying the leaves and stems, and then using them to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
